root_text.sn55.47:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho nandiyaṁ sakkaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.47:1.3: “catūhi kho, nandiya, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo.
root_text.sn55.47:2.2: Idha, nandiya, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.47:2.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.47:2.6: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.47:2.7: Imehi kho, nandiya, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo”ti.

translation_text.sn55.47:1.2: Seated to one side, the Buddha said to Nandiya the Sakyan:
translation_text.sn55.47:1.3: “Nandiya, a noble disciple who has four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.47:2.2: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.47:2.6: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.47:2.7: A noble disciple who has these four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
